title: Wondering why "everything feels divided and why it sucks constantly?" Blame the uni-context. Agnes Callard explains
description: Wondering why "everything feels divided and why it sucks constantly?" Blame the uni-context. Agnes Callard explains
hook: Why everything feels fractured and miserable right now has less to do with politics than with how we've collapsed all contexts into one. Agnes Callard explains the trap.

title: “Heated Rivalry” Is the “Moby-Dick” of Canadian Gay Hockey Shows
description:
The literary genre of contemporary MM romance realizes an imagined world that Melville was trying to write into being.

The post “Heated Rivalry” Is the “Moby-Dick” of Canadian Gay Hockey Shows appeared first on Public Books.
hook: A Canadian hockey romance between two men becomes the great American novel of desire, rivalry, and coded intimacy that Melville couldn't quite write.

title: A City Bigger Than a Bomb: Dispatch from Beirut
description: “For almost a year after October 7, Israel’s war with Lebanon remained an oddly prim, choreographed conflict on the border.”
hook: A Paris Review correspondent watches Beirut navigate the strange choreography of war—prim and restrained until suddenly it isn't.

title: Welcome to Toronto, World Capital of the Urban Raccoon
description: Dan Werb on the understated intelligence of the animals that share our cities.
hook: Raccoons aren't vermin—they're proof that intelligence thrives in the margins of human cities, watching us from the shadows.

title: The looting of science fiction
description:
Tech titans claim the genre inspired them. But all they’ve done is graft their politics onto stories of a better future

- by Ali Rıza Taşkale
hook: Tech billionaires are strip-mining science fiction for ideas, leaving the genre hollowed out and indistinguishable from a pitch deck.
